name: Colorado Dietitian Registration
description: Already licensed as a dietitian? Here's whether your license transfers to Colorado - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

provider.name: State Board of Dietetics and Nutrition (New)
provider.parentOrganization: Colorado Department of Regulatory Agencies (DORA), Division of Professions and Occupations

address.description:
Colorado Department of Regulatory Agencies
Office of Professions and Occupations
1560 Broadway, Suite 1350
Denver, CO 80202

renewalCycle: Annual (Estimated) + 75 CPE hours per 5-year CDR cycle

requiredDocuments.name: CDR Registration Verification
requiredDocuments.description: Proof of active registration with the Commission on Dietetic Registration.

requiredDocuments.name: CBI Background Check Fingerprints
requiredDocuments.description: Colorado Bureau of Investigation background check, processed at a DORA-approved provider.

requiredDocuments.name: Proof of Supervised Practice
requiredDocuments.description: Verification of at least 1,000 hours of supervised practice from an ACEND-accredited program.

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: When does Colorado's dietitian license requirement take effect?

acceptedAnswer.text: The new licensure requirement takes effect on September 1, 2026. After this date, no person may practice or offer medical nutrition therapy without a state license.

mainEntity.name: How much will a Colorado dietitian license cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: The estimated initial registration fee is approximately $200 for FY 2026-27, plus a $39.50 CBI background check. Annual renewal is estimated between $125 and $180.

mainEntity.name: Do I need a license before September 1, 2026?

acceptedAnswer.text: No. Before September 1, 2026, Colorado does not mandate state licensure. The CDR credential (RD/RDN) remains the recognized standard until the new law takes effect.

mainEntity.name: Does Colorado have reciprocity for dietitians?

acceptedAnswer.text: Colorado is currently a CDR State. There is no separate state endorsement transfer pathway right now; maintain an active CDR credential to practice.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to Colorado?

acceptedAnswer.text: This is currently a CDR State, so there is no state transfer timeline. You can usually start once your CDR credential and employer onboarding are complete.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in Colorado while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: There is no active state endorsement application to wait on right now. Start dates are typically driven by employer credentialing and onboarding requirements.
